Title: The cis-prenyltransferase protein family in Taraxacum koksaghyz

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPTL1 interacts with TkCPT1-4 to form heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase complexes. 2. TkCPT1/TkCPTL1 and TkCPT2/TkCPTL1 produced extra-long-chain polyisoprenes in yeast, linked to natural rubber biosynthesis. 3. TkCPT3/TkCPTL1 and TkCPT4/TkCPTL1 restored yeast dolichol function and produced long-chain dolichols/polyisoprenes.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required partner enabling cis-prenyltransferase activity of TkCPT1-4; supports natural rubber and dolichol/polyisoprene formation depending on partner CPT.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC interaction assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast complementation; LC-MS; ThFFF; Western blot
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Natural rubber is an industrial biopolymer; dolichols support protein N-glycosylation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex for natural rubber; various tissues for dolichol/polyisoprenes
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Yeast cultivation conditions including improved low-temperature cultivation for some product analyses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory subunit for engineering heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase complexes to enhance natural rubber or tailored polyisoprenoid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Orthologous/identical to TbRTA from Taraxacum brevicorniculatum in prior work; localized to ER in previous study cited here.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for gene identification; accession NA

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPTL2 interacts with TkCPT3 and TkCPT4 in BiFC assays and had been previously shown to interact with TkCPT1 and TkCPT2. 2. TkCPT3/TkCPTL2 and TkCPT4/TkCPTL2 complemented yeast defective in dolichol biosynthesis. 3. TkCPT2/TkCPTL2 was previously reported as insufficient for functional complementation.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alternative accessory subunit that can activate TkCPT3 and TkCPT4, and interacts with TkCPT1 and TkCPT2, but shows partner-specific functional differences.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BiFC interaction assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast complementation; Western blot
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Dolichol/polyisoprenes; natural rubber-related extra-long polyisoprenes context
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential alternative partner subunit for reconstructing heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase complexes with partner-specific product outputs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Partner specificity is important; TkCPT2/TkCPTL2 was insufficient for complementation according to prior work cited.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain/yeast deletion strains; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: allylic prenyl diphosphate plus IPP; likely trans,trans-FPP preferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-1,4-isoprene chain elongation to produce extra-long-chain polyisoprenes when partnered with TkCPTL1.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGPD in yeast product-analysis constructs; transient expression constructs in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT1 is a heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase subunit localized to the ER. 2. It requires TkCPTL1/2 for activity and cannot complement yeast alone. 3. TkCPT1/TkCPTL1 produced extra-long-chain polyisoprenes in yeast with molecular weights around 10^6, consistent with a natural rubber biosynthetic role.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes natural rubber/extra-long-chain polyisoprene biosynthesis, especially in latex.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in N. benthamiana; prior BiFC; yeast complementation; ThFFF; 1H-NMR; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-temperature yeast cultivation enhanced TkCPT1/TkCPTL1 poly(cis-1,4-isoprene) accumulation in product-analysis experiments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target to improve natural rubber production in T. koksaghyz or heterologous systems, likely together with TkCPTL1 and other rubber transferase machinery.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identical to TbCPT2 from T. brevicorniculatum per cited prior work.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain/yeast deletion strains; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: allylic prenyl diphosphate plus IPP; likely trans,trans-FPP preferred
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-1,4-isoprene chain elongation to produce extra-long-chain polyisoprenes when partnered with TkCPTL1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT2 is a latex-enriched heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase subunit. 2. TkCPT2/TkCPTL1 produced extra-long-chain polyisoprenes in yeast but at lower yield and lower apparent polymer size than TkCPT1/TkCPTL1. 3. Codon harmonization improved expression and product yield in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports natural rubber-related extra-long-chain polyisoprene biosynthesis in latex.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in N. benthamiana; prior BiFC; yeast complementation; ThFFF; Western blot
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower activity/yield than TkCPT1 in yeast-based product assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Improved cultivation conditions and codon harmonization increased detectable product formation in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for natural-rubber engineering, though less productive than TkCPT1 in yeast and partner-specific effects should be considered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TkCPT2/TkCPTL2 was previously insufficient for functional complementation; identical to TbCPT3 from T. brevicorniculatum per cited work.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; yeast deletion strains; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: dolichol/polyisoprenes, mainly C70 in yeast with TkCPTL1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-prenyl chain elongation to form long-chain dolichols/polyisoprenes when partnered with TkCPTL1 or TkCPTL2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT3 is an ER-associated heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase subunit strongly expressed in leaves. 2. It interacts with TkCPTL1 and TkCPTL2 and complements yeast dolichol deficiency only when co-expressed with a TkCPTL partner. 3. TkCPT3/TkCPTL1 produced long-chain dolichol/polyisoprenes, predominantly C70, and restored full CPY glycosylation in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes long-chain dolichol/polyisoprene biosynthesis linked to proper N-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; localization assay; BiFC; yeast complementation; Western blot; LC-MS; CPY glycosylation assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required as lipid carriers for protein N-glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Latex, root, leaf, peduncle, flower polyisoprenoid pools; exact TkCPT3-specific site inferred mainly leaf
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering dolichol/polyisoprenoid biosynthesis and studying glycosylation-related polyisoprenoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not function with yeast Nus1, indicating species-dependent CPT-CPTL compatibility.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; yeast deletion strains; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: dolichol/polyisoprenes, mainly C80 in yeast with TkCPTL1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes cis-prenyl chain elongation to form long-chain dolichols/polyisoprenes when partnered with TkCPTL1 or TkCPTL2.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT4 is an ER-associated heteromeric cis-prenyltransferase subunit with low constitutive expression. 2. It interacts with TkCPTL1 and TkCPTL2 and complements yeast dolichol deficiency only with a TkCPTL partner. 3. TkCPT4/TkCPTL1 produced longer dolichol/polyisoprene chains than TkCPT3, mainly C80, and restored full CPY glycosylation in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes long-chain dolichol/polyisoprene biosynthesis supporting N-glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; localization assay; BiFC; yeast complementation; Western blot; LC-MS; CPY glycosylation assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required as lipid carriers for protein N-glycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for tuning long-chain dolichol product profiles in metabolic engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Did not function with yeast Nus1, supporting species-specific partner requirement.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain; NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes synthesis of the short-chain polyisoprenoid pentaprenol as a homomeric cis-prenyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGPD in yeast product-analysis constructs using transit-peptide-truncated gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT5 is a plastid-localized homomeric cis-prenyltransferase highly expressed in flowers. 2. It produced pentaprenol in yeast, and the same compound was detected in T. koksaghyz flowers. 3. TkCPT5 did not complement yeast dolichol-deficient mutants, consistent with too-short products for glycosylation support.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pentaprenol accumulation, especially in flowers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; localization assay; BiFC negative assay; yeast complementation; GC-MS; LC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Biological role unknown; may influence plastid membrane functions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Fails to restore yeast glycosylation/growth as dolichol substitute due to short unsaturated product
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for engineering unusual short-chain prenols such as pentaprenol.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Product appears novel/specific in flowers; study reports first evidence of this pentaprenol in T. koksaghyz.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain/yeast deletion strains; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: hexaprenol to decaprenol (C30-C50); nonaprenol detected in planta
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes homomeric cis-prenyl chain elongation producing medium-chain prenols from hexaprenol to decaprenol.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT6 is a likely plastidial homomeric cis-prenyltransferase strongly expressed in leaves. 2. Unlike TkCPT5/7/8, TkCPT6 alone complemented yeast cis-prenyltransferase deficiency, indicating autonomous activity. 3. It produced hexaprenol to decaprenol in yeast, while nonaprenol was the only matching product detected in planta; complementation restored growth but not full protein glycosylation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes medium-chain polyisoprenoid accumulation, likely contributing to nonaprenol/solanesol-related metabolism in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; localization assay; yeast complementation; Western blot; LC-MS; CPY glycosylation assay
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: May serve as precursor for plastoquinone-9 or ubiquinone-9 and may influence plastid membrane properties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Restores yeast growth but causes hypoglycosylation, indicating incomplete functional substitution for native dolichol
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering solanesol/nonaprenol-related plastidial polyisoprenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only homomeric TkCPT that complemented yeast deletion strains in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain; NA
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely DMAPP plus IPP or short allylic substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of the cis-C10 prenyl product NPP, detected as nerol in yeast extracts.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGPD in yeast product-analysis constructs using transit-peptide-truncated gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT7 is a plastidial homomeric cis-prenyltransferase highly expressed in leaves. 2. It produced nerol in yeast, interpreted as the dephosphorylated form of NPP. 3. Nerol was not detected in planta, suggesting the product serves as a precursor for monoterpenes or further metabolism rather than accumulating end-product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies cis-monoterpene precursor pool rather than accumulating stable prenols.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; localization assay; BiFC negative assay; yeast complementation; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely precursor for monoterpenes or other specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves as inferred precursor activity; nerol not detected in plant tissues tested
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not complement yeast dolichol deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential entry point for engineering cis-monoterpene precursor supply in plastids.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alanine at position 337 supports short-chain-product tendency.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Taraxacum koksaghyz; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild type; engineered yeast strain; NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes formation of a short cis-C10 prenyl product, likely NPP, detected as nerol in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Not detected experimentally; predicted chloroplast transit peptide
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PGPD in yeast product-analysis constructs using transit-peptide-truncated gene
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TkCPT8 is a putative homomeric short-chain cis-prenyltransferase expressed most strongly in leaves. 2. It produced nerol in yeast, implying NPP synthesis. 3. It did not complement yeast mutants and may have lower activity associated with the missing canonical RXG motif.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supplies short cis-prenyl precursor for downstream monoterpene or related metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; BiFC negative assay; yeast complementation; GC-MS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely precursor for monoterpenes or other compounds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves as inferred precursor activity; nerol not detected in plant tissues tested
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Does not complement yeast dolichol deficiency; lower activity may result from missing RXG motif
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tool for studying and engineering short cis-prenyl precursor formation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted to prefer DMAPP due to active-site substitution; fluorescence signal not detected in localization assay.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq used for family identification; accession NA
